sections.paragraph:
λάθρᾳ adv. (on the spelling with or without ι s. B-D-F §26; W-S. §5, 11c; Mlt-H. 84; Boisacq 549) (s. λανθάνω; Hom. [λάθρῃ] et al.; SIG 609, 6 [?]; UPZ 19, 28 [163 b.c.]; BGU 1141, 48; TestSol; TestJud 5:4).

① (to do someth.) without others being aware, secretly, used as adv. (Diod S 11, 88, 4; 11, 91, 2; Dt 13:7 λ. λέγειν; 1 Macc 9:60; Jos., Bell. 2, 408) opp. φανερῶς (TestSol 4:6; Diod S 16, 24, 2; Appian, Bell. Civ. 3, 91 §376; Cass. Dio 69, 13, 1) IPhld 6:3; cp. GMary 463, 13; ἀπολύειν Mt 1:19 (so also GJs 14:1). ἐκβάλλειν Ac 16:37. καλεῖν (Jos., Vi. 388) Mt 2:7. ποιεῖν Mk 5:33 v.l. φωνεῖν J 11:28. λαλεῖν GMary 463, 13.

② (to do someth.) without going through proper channels, without the knowledge of, behind the back of, as prep. w. gen. (Hom.+; Diod S 5, 65, 4 λ. τοῦ πατρός; Sb 6222, 17; Ps 100:5 λ. τοῦ πλησίον αὐτοῦ) λ. ἐπισκόπου ISm 9:1.—DELG s.v. λανθάνω. M-M. Spicq.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

W-S. W-S. = GWiner, Grammatik des neutestamentlichen Sprachidioms, ed. PSchmiedel—List 6

Mlt-H. Mlt-H. = JMoulton/WHoward, A Grammar of NT Greek II: Accidence and Word-formation—List 6

Boisacq Boisacq = ÉB., Dictionnaire étymologique de la Langue Greque—List 6

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

someth. someth. = something

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

IPhld IPhld = Ignatius to the Philadelphians—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

prep. prep. = preposition(al)

gen. gen. = genitive

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 581
